name: Kimi K2.6
archDisplay: 1T-parameter MoE, 32B active/token, 384 experts (8 routed + 1 shared), 61 layers (1 dense), MLA attention, 160K vocab, SwiGLU; native multimodal via 400M-param MoonViT vision encoder
note: Successor to K2.5; Moonshot's flagship open-weight agentic/coding model, extends context to 256K, adds native image/video input, and scales its 'Agent Swarm' orchestration to 300 sub-agents / ~4,000 coordinated steps. Vendor claims top-of-open-weights on SWE-bench Verified and near-parity with GPT-5.5/Claude Opus 4.6-class closed models on several coding/agentic suites; independent Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index score was 44 (v4.1 methodology), and LMArena text arena placed it around #7 overall (Elo ~1522) as the top open-weight entrant at the time — both corroborate strong but not universally frontier-topping performance versus the more selective vendor benchmark set.

results.notes: SWE-bench Pro, harder/agentic variant distinct from base swe-bench
MMMU-Pro standard; vendor also reports 80.1 with python tool use enabled
vendor also reports 86.3 in an 'agent swarm' multi-agent configuration; 83.2 is the single-agent figure used here for comparability
Humanity's Last Exam, full set, with tool use enabled
CharXiv (RQ), no python tool use, avg@3, max-tokens=98,304; vendor also reports 86.7 with python tool use enabled. From the official Kimi K2.6 tech blog benchmark table.
MathVision, no python tool use, avg@3; vendor also reports 93.2 with python tool use enabled.
OSWorld-Verified, from the official Kimi K2.6 tech blog Agentic benchmark table (Kimi K2.5's own OSWorld-Verified score of 63.3 is already recorded on the sibling K2.5 corpus entry).
Agents' Last Exam (ALE-CLI, Overall leaderboard), OpenClaw harness, High reasoning effort: Pass rate 9.2% / Score 21.7% (rank 45). Score component used per corpus convention.

GDPval-AA v2 Elo = 1190.39 for Kimi K2.6, confirmed directly from the leaderboard page's embedded structured dataset (schema.org Dataset JSON-LD), independently measured by Artificial Analysis ('measurementTechnique: Independent test run by Artificial Analysis'). Note: Artificial Analysis's own launch-week article ('Kimi K2.6: The new leading open weights model') separately cites a higher Elo of 1520 for K2.6 at launch; the live number has since drifted down to 1190 as many more/stronger models (Claude Fable 5, GPT-5.6 Sol, etc.) were added to the comparison pool -- normal behavior for a continuously-updating relative Elo leaderboard anchored to a fixed human baseline. Used the current live figure since it's the one directly verifiable in the page's own dataset. GDPval-AA was previously only referenced narratively (as an Intelligence-Index component) in this model's notes/verificationNotes text, never as a discrete results-array entry.

Arena.ai Text Arena Overall Elo for 'kimi-k2.6' -- rank 37/374, 36,683 votes, $0.95/$4 pricing and 262.1K context shown alongside it, all matching this model's price/ctx fields. Confirmed via the leaderboard's own embedded data table (Jul 13, 2026 snapshot). Previously only referenced narratively in this model's note text (~1522 launch-week Elo); this adds it as a discrete, currently-verified result.

verificationNotes:
Confirmed real, released model (not a rumor). Cross-verified architecture/param counts (1T total/32B active/384 experts/61 layers/MLA/MoonViT) across the official Hugging Face model card and the official Kimi blog post (kimi.com/blog/kimi-k2-6). Release date April 20, 2026 corroborated by SiliconANGLE, MarkTechPost, Yicai Global, and the kimi-k2.org mirror; HF repo's own lastModified timestamp (2026-05-19) reflects a later card update, not the release date. Pricing $0.95/M input (cache miss) and $4.00/M output confirmed directly on Moonshot's own hosted platform (both platform.kimi.ai and artificialanalysis.ai's tracked listing agree). Independent corroboration beyond vendor self-reports: Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index = 44/100 (composite of GDPval-AA v2, tau3-Banking, Terminal-Bench v2.1, SciCode, HLE, GPQA-Diamond, CritPt, AA-Omniscience, AA-LCR) and an LMArena text-arena placement around #7 overall / Elo ~1522, both independently measured and both broadly consistent with (though less flattering than) Moonshot's own benchmark table.

2026-07-16 core-benchmark gap check (mmlu, math/MATH-500, humaneval, mmmu-plain): none of these four are published for Kimi K2.6. Checked (1) official launch materials -- the kimi.com tech blog (kimi.com/blog/kimi-k2-6) and the Hugging Face model card (huggingface.co/moonshotai/Kimi-K2.6) -- both carry the same ~30-row benchmark table spanning agentic, coding, reasoning, and vision suites, and neither includes MMLU, MATH-500, HumanEval, or plain (non-Pro) MMMU; (2) independent leaderboards -- Artificial Analysis's evaluation-component list for K2.6 (artificialanalysis.ai/models/kimi-k2-6: GDPval-AA v2, tau3-Banking, Terminal-Bench v2.1, SciCode, HLE, GPQA-Diamond, CritPt, AA-Omniscience, AA-LCR, plus AA-Briefcase/AutomationBench/Harvey-LAB/EnterpriseOps-Gym/IFBench/APEX-Agents/ITBench/MMMU-Pro) likewise omits all four; Epoch AI's Kimi K2.6 model page (epoch.ai/models/kimi-k2-6) exposes only its aggregate Epoch Capabilities Index (151, rank 25/211) with no per-benchmark breakdown retrievable via static fetch, and Vals.ai's Kimi K2.6 page (vals.ai/models/kimi_kimi-k2.6) lists MMLU Pro and MMMU (not plain MMLU/MATH/HumanEval) among its 24 tracked evals but the per-benchmark percentage values render client-side only and could not be reliably extracted (JS-animated counters, not present in server HTML); and (3) sibling-model cross-reference within this corpus -- Kimi K2.5, Kimi K2-Thinking, and Kimi K2.7-code (all released after original Kimi K2) likewise carry no mmlu/math/humaneval/mmmu(plain) entries, reporting only the harder successor metrics mmlu-pro, mmlu-redux, and mmmu-pro instead, versus the original Kimi K2 and Kimi K1.5 (older releases) which did report mmlu/math/mmmu. This is a consistent, dated pattern of Moonshot dropping saturated legacy benchmarks starting with the K2.5/K2-Thinking generation, not a research gap specific to K2.6; treating the absence as genuine and omitting all four rather than estimating.

2026-07-17 second-pass (round-2) gap chase for mmlu, math, humaneval -- explicitly using source types NOT covered by the 2026-07-16 first pass (official launch materials, Epoch AI, Vals.ai, LMArena, BenchLM). Checked all 6 prescribed new source types directly (primary-source fetches, not just search-engine summaries, several of which proved unreliable/hallucination-prone for this model -- see spam note below):

(1) Stanford HELM -- verified via the stanford-crfm/helm GitHub repo's own CHANGELOG.md: Moonshot/Kimi support was added in v0.5.6/v0.5.7 (2025-06/07, plain "Kimi K2") and v0.5.12 (2026-01-27, "Kimi K2 Instruct 0905"). No changelog entry adds Kimi K2.5 or K2.6 at any point through the latest release. Since Kimi K2.6 released 2026-04-20 -- after HELM's last Kimi-related changelog entry -- HELM has never evaluated it. Ruled out with high confidence.

(2) arXiv / Semantic Scholar -- read the Kimi K2 technical report (arxiv.org/abs/2507.20534, predates K2.6, reports MMLU 89.5 for the original Kimi-K2-Instruct only) and the Kimi K2.5 technical report (arxiv.org/abs/2602.02276, predates K2.6, no K2.6 mention). Searched for any paper citing "Kimi K2.6" as a comparison baseline; one candidate hit (arxiv.org/abs/2605.26934 / pdf/2605.26934) was fetched in full and confirmed to mention only "Kimi K2" in its references section with zero benchmark scores attached. No arXiv or Semantic Scholar paper reporting a real K2.6 MMLU/MATH/HumanEval number was found.

(3) GitHub eval-harness run logs -- github.com/topics/kimi-k2-6 lists only deployment/tooling repos (a desktop-agent wrapper, a cookbook, an analog-design agent), no eval-results/lm-evaluation-harness repos. GitHub code search requires authentication and could not be queried directly. No evidence found via available search.

(4) Other leaderboards/aggregators -- OpenRouter's dedicated Kimi K2.6 benchmarks page (openrouter.ai/moonshotai/kimi-k2.6/benchmarks) shows only pricing/context/uptime, no MMLU/MATH/HumanEval. llm-stats.com's Kimi K2.6 page explicitly renders "Loading chart..." placeholders under its Rankings/Quality Tracker sections -- confirmed via direct fetch that MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval appear only as unpopulated leaderboard-category links in the footer nav, not as scored results. Scale AI's SEAL leaderboard (Seal-0) currently only has Kimi K2.5 among Moonshot models, not K2.6. PapersWithCode-style queries surfaced no legitimate populated entry (see spam note below).

(5) Non-English sources -- ran Chinese and Japanese-language searches. Directly fetched/read (as PDF) a Japanese 4-model comparison report (yorozuipsc.com, "中国LLM四モデル比較レポート", dated 2026-04-26 JST) that looked like an independent analyst writeup; on inspection it is an AI-generated synthesis (bears a "ChatGPT" header) whose Kimi K2.6 row cites only huggingface.co/moonshotai/Kimi-K2.6, kimi.com/blog, and artificialanalysis.ai/models/kimi-k2-6 -- the exact same primary sources already exhausted in the first pass -- and reproduces only HLE/BrowseComp/DeepSearchQA/Terminal-Bench/SWE-Bench/AIME/GPQA/HMMT, no MMLU/MATH/HumanEval. Other Chinese search results were spam (see below).

(6) Sibling-model cross-reference -- Kimi K2.7-Code comparison coverage (MarkTechPost and others) uses only Moonshot-specific coding suites (Kimi Code Bench v2, Program Bench, MLS Bench Lite, Kimi Claw 24/7 Bench, MCP Atlas/Mark Verified), never MMLU/MATH/HumanEval, consistent with the K2.5-generation-onward pattern already documented. Kimi K3 (announced ~2026-07-16/17, too new) has no technical report or populated comparison table yet.

Side-finding worth flagging for future research rounds: multiple SEO/AI-content-farm sites (neuraplus-ai.github.io, kimik3.xyz, binaryverseai.com, morphllm.com, layerlens.ai, flowtivity.ai, codesota.com, automatio.ai) are independently circulating an identical fabricated figure set -- "MMLU 78.2 / MATH 68.5 / HumanEval 79.1" -- attributed to "Kimi K2" or "Kimi K2.6" interchangeably. Directly fetched the apparent origin (neuraplus-ai.github.io/blog/kimi-k2-6-benchmark.html): the page actually scores plain "Kimi K2" (not K2.6), against already-obsolete comparison models (GPT-4, Claude 3.5 Sonnet, Gemini 1.5 Pro, Llama 3.1 405B) that no legitimate 2026 benchmark article would use as its comparison set, with a boilerplate "we tested... temperature 0.7, top-p 0.9" methodology claim and zero external citations -- strong evidence of template-generated/fabricated content, not a real evaluation. None of these numbers were used. This also explains why several WebSearch tool-call summaries during this pass surfaced inconsistent/wrong numbers (e.g. one summary asserted an Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index of "54" for K2.6, contradicting this corpus's already-verified value of 44) -- underscoring that this model's benchmark-name search results are unusually contaminated by AI-generated spam, and that direct primary-source fetches (not search-summary trust) were essential for this round.

Conclusion: mmlu, math, and humaneval remain genuinely unpublished for Kimi K2.6 across all 6 newly-checked source-type categories, in addition to the 5 source types already exhausted in the 2026-07-16 first pass. Omitting all three again as confirmed absences, not estimates.
